questions.question: The Eastern Bridge-VI exercise is conducted at which Indian city?
questions.explanation: The ‘Eastern Bridge-VI’, which is a defence exercise between the Air Forces of India and Oman is conducted from 21 to 25 February 2022 at Air Force Station Jodhpur. This is the 6th edition of the bilateral exercise which is aimed to bolster operational efficiency of both the air warriors and improve interoperability between the two Air Forces.

questions.question: When is the ‘Central Excise Day’ observed every year in India?
questions.explanation: ‘Central Excise Day’ is observed every year on February 24 in India. It commemorates the Central Excise and Salt Act which was enacted on 24th February 1944. Central Excise Day honours the contribution of the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) in the industrial development of the country.

questions.question: What is the name of the Asteroid that is found to have three Moons?
questions.explanation: A team of Thailand and French astronomers found an asteroid system with three satellites, named (130) Elektra. The asteroid was first found in 1873 and it measured about 200 km in diameter and weighs approximately 7 x 10^18 kg. It has captured its three orbiting smaller asteroids, known as S/2003 (130) 1, S/2014 (130)1, and S/2014 (130)2.

questions.question: India has agreed to establish an Indian Institute of Technology in which country?
questions.explanation: India and UAE signed the historic Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) to boost the trade to US$ 100 billion over next five years. As a part of the agreement, the leaders agreed to establish an Indian Institute of Technology in the United Arab Emirates. The IITs are governed by the Institutes of Technology Act, 1961 and there are 23 IITs in India, at present.

questions.question: Euphlyctis jaladhara, which has been discovered along the Western Ghats, belongs to which species?
questions.explanation: A study by the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI), Pune, described the discovery of a new frog species growing in freshwater sources along all the states spanned by Western Ghats. Euphlyctis jaladhara, or Jaladhara skittering frog, was spotted during the forest surveys focusing on freshwater bodies in Karnataka, Goa, Maharashtra, Gujarat and UT of Daman and Diu, and Dadra and Nagar Haveli.

questions.question: The Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) Scheme was launched un which year?
questions.explanation: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) Scheme was launched 24th of February in 2019 to provide income support to all landholding farmer’s families. Under the Scheme, an amount of Rs 6000 per year is transferred in three equal installments of two thousand rupees directly into the bank accounts of the farmers. Over 1.80 lakh crore rupees have been transferred directly, under PM KISAN scheme.

questions.question: The Government of which state / UT has approved a new Film Policy for 2022?
questions.explanation: The Government of Delhi has approved a new Film Policy, 2022 – which aims to promote Delhi as a hub for filming and other related activities. The policy provides for setting up of Rs 50 crore ‘Delhi Film Fund’ to support producers and host international film festivals. The policy also envisages a subsidy of up to Rs 3 crore to the industry.

questions.question: What is the revised growth forecast for India by Moody’s Investors Service for FY 2022-23?
questions.explanation: Moody’s Investors Service has revised India’ GDP growth forecast from 7.9% to 8.4% for the financial year 2022-23. Moody’s has estimated India’s growth to be “stronger-than-expected” which is supported by monetary policy and budget. But the rating has cautioned that high oil prices and supply distortions would act as a drag to growth of India.

questions.question: Meitei is a major community demanding for Scheduled Tribe (ST) in which state?
questions.explanation: The majority Meitei community has been demanding Scheduled Tribe (ST) status in Manipur. They are dominant in two-third of the 60 constituencies across Imphal Valley. Although most Meiteis are Hindus, they still follow the old tribal system. Till 1935, Meiteis were entered in the census of India as ST. However, there are also opinions in the communities against the demand of ST status.

questions.question: Which Indian public sector bank has partnered with NPCI to launch ‘MSME RuPay Credit Card’?
questions.explanation: Union Bank of India, in association with National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI), has launched ‘Union MSME RuPay Credit Card’. This card comes with interest free credit up to 50 days, which is used for meeting business related operational expenses of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs). The dedicated card for MSMEs is available to eligible borrowers of Union Bank of India. The card also offers EMI facility on their business-related purchases.
